title: salix drummondiana subsp. subcoerulea
intro: Salix drummondiana subsp. subcoerulea, also known as Drummond's willow or blue-leaved willow, is a subspecies of willow tree native to North America, characterized by its blue-green leaves and slender branches. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever. The health benefits of Salix drummondiana subsp. subcoerulea include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
